verdict.answer_first: B states the plan in the first paragraph ("I'll give you the plan for the most common case"), while A starts with a fabricated blocker.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to a specific technology (SSE) and architecture from the start ("The recommended shape... push them to open tabs over Server-Sent Events (SSE)"), while A presents a choice table first.
verdict.fabrication: A invents a repo name the user never provided: "this repo (`fable-profile`) isn't your web app".
verdict.actionability: B is more actionable because it explains the critical backend decision between single-server and serverless deployments ("in-memory won't work because the writer and the open connection live in different processes"), a key detail A misses.
